description: How to create a brand new Git repository on GitHub.
steps: Log in to GitHub and click the '+' icon in the top-right corner.
Enter a repository name and an optional description.
Choose to make the repository Public or Private.

description: Proposing changes to a repository by creating a pull request.
steps: Navigate to the main page of the repository.
In the 'Branch' menu, choose the branch that contains your commits.
Above the file list, click 'Compare & pull request'.
Type a title and description for your pull request.

description: How to create a new GitHub Actions workflow to automate CI/CD.
steps: Navigate to your repository on GitHub.
Click on the 'Actions' tab under your repository name.
Search for a workflow template that matches your project, or click 'set up a workflow yourself'.
Edit the workflow YAML file according to your automation needs.
Click 'Commit changes' to save the workflow to your `.github/workflows` directory.
